ui.subtitle: Discover the world of money, value, and Bitcoin!
ui.newPlayerConfirm: Start a new player? The current progress will be erased.

sort.instruction: Drag from cheapest to most expensive

cipher.instruction: Crack the code using the cipher wheel

modules.title: Exchange, Value & Price
modules.summary: Value depends on what you need. Price is how much something costs. Money solves the problem of trading.
modules.description: Discover why we use money and what things are really worth!

activities.title: Island Survival
activities.intro: You're a castaway on a deserted island!

questions.q: You wash up on a deserted island. What is worth the most?
questions.explanation: On an island, a machete lets you chop wood, cut food, and build a shelter. Money and diamonds are useless there!

questions.q: You have 5 fishing rods but no tent. Your neighbor has 3 tents but no fishing rod. What do you do?
options: Trade: a fishing rod for a tent!
questions.explanation: Trading is the oldest way to do business! You give what you have extra of in exchange for what you need.

questions.q: Why is trading sometimes really difficult?
options: Because you have to find someone who wants exactly what you have
questions.explanation: This is called the 'double coincidence of wants'. Money solves this problem — everyone accepts money!

questions.q: Shells were once used as money. What makes something good money?
options: It has to be scarce, portable, and accepted by everyone
It has to come from the government
questions.explanation: Good money is scarce (not endlessly available), easy to carry, and everyone must accept it as payment.

questions.q: A bottle of water costs €1 at the supermarket. In the desert, someone offers you €100 for the same bottle. Why?
options: Because water in the desert is much scarcer and more important
questions.explanation: Scarcity changes value! The less there is of something and the more you need it, the more it's worth.

questions.q: What is the difference between price and value?
options: Price is how much something costs, value is how important something is to you
Price is always higher than value
questions.explanation: A drawing made by your child might have no price but enormous value. Price and value are not the same thing!

activities.title: Price Line
activities.intro: Put the items in the correct order from cheapest to most expensive!

modules.title: Time, Energy & Reward
modules.summary: Energy is limited. Balance between work, rest, and play is important. Patience leads to better results.
modules.description: How do you use your time and energy? And why is waiting sometimes smarter?

activities.title: Work, Rest, or Play?
activities.intro: Drag each activity to the correct category!

activities.title: Time Preference
activities.intro: Do you choose NOW or LATER?

questions.q: In the story of The Three Little Pigs: which house was left standing?
questions.explanation: The brick house took the most time and effort, but it was the only one left standing. Patience pays off!

questions.q: What does 'low time preference' mean?
options: You are willing to wait for something better
questions.explanation: Low time preference = being willing to wait for a better outcome. Just like the pig who built a house of bricks!

questions.q: You get 1 candy now, or 3 candies if you wait 10 minutes. What is the smartest choice?
questions.explanation: This is the famous marshmallow test! Kids who could wait often did better in school later on.

questions.q: You have an important test tomorrow. What is low time preference?
options: Studying today so you do well tomorrow
questions.explanation: Doing something hard now (studying) so you get something good later (a good grade) — that's low time preference.

questions.q: What happens if you use up all your energy in the morning?
options: You have nothing left for the rest of the day
questions.explanation: Energy is limited! Spreading it out wisely is important — just like money.

modules.title: Analog versus Digital
modules.summary: Technology keeps changing. Every invention solves a problem. Not everything new is automatically better.
modules.description: From carrier pigeons to the internet — how technology changes the world!

activities.title: Old ↔ New
activities.intro: Match each old technology to its modern version!

activities.title: Imagine the Future
activities.intro: What might technology look like in 20 years?

questions.q: Why do technologies change over time?
options: Because people solve problems with new inventions
questions.explanation: Every invention solves a problem. The car solved the problem of horses being slow and getting tired!

questions.q: Is new technology ALWAYS better than old technology?
options: No, sometimes the old way is better or more reliable
questions.explanation: A paper book doesn't need a battery! Sometimes old solutions are still really smart.

questions.q: Bitcoin is digital money. What does it have in common with old shells?
options: They both come from the sea
Both are scarce and used as a means of exchange
questions.explanation: Both shells and Bitcoin are scarce (there is a limited amount) and are used as a means of exchange.

questions.q: What is innovation?
options: A clever improvement or invention that solves a problem
questions.explanation: Innovation = a new or better solution to an existing problem. Anyone can innovate!

modules.title: Safety & Privacy
modules.summary: You decide what information you share. Codes protect messages. Trust isn't always needed when you have encryption.
modules.description: Secret codes, privacy, and how to protect yourself online!

activities.title: Public or Private?
activities.intro: Is this information public or private? Decide fast!

statements.label: Private!
statements.explanation: A password is always private. Never share it!

statements.label: Public
statements.explanation: At school everyone knows your name — that's public information.

statements.label: Private!
statements.explanation: Your PIN is secret — it protects your bank account.

statements.label: Public
statements.explanation: A park is a public place — anyone can go there.

statements.label: Private!
statements.explanation: Your diary is private — only you decide who gets to read it.

statements.label: Public
statements.explanation: Anyone can see what color your bike is.

statements.label: Private!
statements.explanation: You don't share your address with strangers — that's private information.

statements.label: Public
statements.explanation: The weather is the same for everyone — completely public!

statements.label: Private!
statements.explanation: Your private key is the most important secret of all. Whoever has it controls your bitcoin!

activities.title: Secret Code
activities.intro: Crack the secret Bitcoin words using the cipher wheel!

modules.title: Money Matters
modules.summary: You can earn, save, invest, and spend money. Budgeting means making choices. Fair rules are important.
modules.description: Earning, saving, investing — become a money expert!

activities.title: Smart with Money
activities.intro: Test how smart you are with money!

questions.q: What is saving?
questions.explanation: Saving = keeping money so you can buy something bigger or more important later.

questions.q: What is investing?
options: Putting your money or time into something so it becomes worth more later
questions.explanation: Investing means putting your money or time into something with the hope that it will be worth more later.

questions.q: You have 12 shells. A tent costs 8 and a fishing rod costs 6. Can you buy both?
questions.explanation: 8 + 6 = 14, but you only have 12 shells. You have to choose! That's budgeting.

questions.q: At an auction, the price of a rare item keeps going up. Why?
options: Multiple people want it, but there's only one (scarcity + demand)
questions.explanation: When many people want something but there's very little of it, the price goes up. That's how supply and demand works!

questions.q: Why are fair rules about money important?
options: So that everyone gets the same chance
questions.explanation: Fair rules make sure no one can cheat and everyone has the same chance.

questions.q: What is borrowing?
options: Getting money from someone else that you have to pay back later
questions.explanation: Borrowing means you get money from someone else, but you have to pay it back later — often with extra (interest).

modules.title: Bitcoin Basics
modules.summary: Bitcoin is scarce digital money without a middleman. Mining confirms transactions. Satoshi remained anonymous.
modules.description: The great Bitcoin adventure: from Satoshi to mining!

questions.q: Who invented Bitcoin?
questions.explanation: Satoshi Nakamoto published a 'white paper' in 2008 with the idea for Bitcoin. Nobody knows who Satoshi really is!

questions.q: What is a 'white paper'?
options: A document in which you explain a new idea
questions.explanation: A white paper is a document in which you present a new idea or solution to the world.

questions.q: What is the Genesis Block?
options: The very first block of Bitcoin
questions.explanation: On January 3, 2009, the Genesis Block was created — the very first block in the Bitcoin blockchain.

questions.q: How many bitcoins will ever exist?
questions.explanation: There will never be more than 21 million bitcoins. That scarcity is built into the rules!

questions.q: What is 'mining' in Bitcoin?
options: Using computers to solve puzzles to confirm transactions
questions.explanation: Miners use computers to solve mathematical puzzles. As proof that they did the work, they get to confirm transactions.

questions.q: What is Proof of Work?
options: Proof that someone really put in effort to earn something
questions.explanation: Proof of Work = proof of work done. Just like a dice game: everyone follows the same rules and has the same chance.

questions.q: Why did Satoshi disappear?
options: Probably to make Bitcoin truly belong to everyone, without a leader
questions.explanation: By disappearing, Satoshi made sure Bitcoin doesn't belong to one person. The network runs on rules, not on a leader.

activities.title: Bitcoin Mining!
activities.intro: Find matching pairs to mine blocks — just like real miners!

words.hint: This is where you store your bitcoin
